Diversify-Aggregate-Repeat Training algorithm (DART) is a proposed method that aims at exploring the basin of solutions effectively with a robust optimization path and combines the expertise of several diverse models. A series of observations from prior works have led to the conjecture that models trained independently with different initializations could be linearly connected with a low loss barrier, when different permutations of their weights are considered, suggesting that all solutions effectively lie in a common basin.

The input for the DART algorithm consists of M networks fθk, where 0 < k ≤ M, and their corresponding datasets Dk. The union of all datasets is denoted as D∗. The model is trained using Empirical Risk Minimization (ERM) initially for E epochs.

The goal of the Diversify-Aggregate-Repeat Training algorithm (DART) is to aggregate the weights of multiple networks every λ epochs to obtain a single generalized solution. This process enables the combination of knowledge from different models to converge towards a more refined single solution.

DART - Diversify-Aggregate-Repeat Training aims to achieve the benefits of training diverse experts by using a combination of diverse augmentations within a training minibatch.
The proposed algorithm first trains several models on different augmentations to learn a diverse set of features, and further aggregates their weights to obtain better generalization.

The steps of Diversify-Aggregate are repeated several times over training to make the optimization trajectory more robust by ensuring a low loss barrier between the individual models and enabling their effective aggregation for better generalization.
DART - Diversify-Aggregate-Repeat Training suppresses the learning of noisy features to make the optimization trajectory more robust.
